questiontext.text:
Consider the two figures shown on the coordinate grid.

[[jsxgraph]] var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, { boundingbox: [-10,8,10,-8], axis:true, showCopyright:false }); /* Original triangle */ var A = board.create('point', [{#Ax#},{#Ay#}], {name:'A', size:3}); var B = board.create('point', [{#Bx#},{#By#}], {name:'B', size:3}); var C = board.create('point', [{#Cx#},{#Cy#}], {name:'C', size:3}); board.create('polygon',[A,B,C],{ fillColor:'#a5d6a7', fillOpacity:0.6, borders:{strokeWidth:2} }); /* Image triangle */ var S = board.create('point', [{#Sx#},{#Sy#}], {name:'S', size:3}); var T = board.create('point', [{#Tx#},{#Ty#}], {name:'T', size:3}); var V = board.create('point', [{#Vx#},{#Vy#}], {name:'V', size:3}); board.create('polygon',[S,T,V],{ fillColor:'#90caf9', fillOpacity:0.6, borders:{strokeWidth:2} }); [[/jsxgraph]]

a) Are the two figures congruent?
[[input:ans1]] [[validation:ans1]] [[feedback:prt1]]

b) If they are congruent, describe the transformation that maps the first figure onto the second.
[[input:ans2]] [[validation:ans2]] [[feedback:prt2]]

generalfeedback.text:
Worked Solution

Two figures in the plane are congruent if one can be obtained from the other by a rigid transformation (also called an isometry).

Rigid transformations preserve:

  • All side lengths
  • All angles
  • The overall shape and size of the figure

The main rigid transformations in the plane are:

  • Translation – shifting every point by the same vector
  • Rotation – turning the figure around a fixed point
  • Reflection – flipping the figure across a line

Any combination of these transformations is still rigid.

How to analyze this problem:

  1. Check whether the side lengths of the two triangles are the same. If distances are preserved, congruence is possible.
  2. Determine the type of transformation:
    • If every vertex is shifted by the same vector → it is a translation.
    • If the triangle appears turned (possibly together with a shift) → it is a rotation and translation.
    • If the triangle is stretched (for example, one coordinate is scaled differently) → distances are not preserved and the figures are not congruent.

Remember:

Congruent figures have exactly the same shape and size.

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.

qtest.description: Test case assuming the teacher's input gets full marks.
